design_decisions.thmsmith_propose_topic: Weak-quotient inference phase for proximal bridge target uniqueness. Pre-anchor check: closest anchors are the Zhang-Li-Miao-Tchetgen Tchetgen null-annihilator criterion for nonunique bridges, proximal AIPW bridge estimation papers, and weak-identification/singular-matrix inference. Why non-trivial? The target is not the already-solved algebraic quotient criterion and not another completeness threshold: require a fixed-rank finite negative-control model where the bridge operator and target loading are estimated, and derive a local-to-nullspace phase theorem for the plug-in quotient diagnostic. The nonroutine object is a cone-angle certificate: when the target-loading distance to the estimated bridge nullspace is separated from zero, ordinary Gaussian inference is valid; when it is n^{-1/2}-local, the diagnostic has a nonstandard projected-Gaussian/chi-bar-square law and naive quotient-identification decisions have nonvanishing size distortion. Why promising? This is estimator geometry around a published identification criterion, with a two-matrix hand witness and explicit phase boundary, not LP duality or support coverage. If the delta reduces to standard singular-value perturbation, generic weak-IV analogies, path derivatives, or rephrasing the Zhang nullspace iff, pivot or accept field-tier.

proposed_from.topic: Weak-quotient inference phase for proximal bridge target uniqueness. Pre-anchor check: closest anchors are the Zhang-Li-Miao-Tchetgen Tchetgen null-annihilator criterion for nonunique bridges, proximal AIPW bridge estimation papers, and weak-identification/singular-matrix inference. Why non-trivial? The target is not the already-solved algebraic quotient criterion and not another completeness threshold: require a fixed-rank finite negative-control model where the bridge operator and target loading are estimated, and derive a local-to-nullspace phase theorem for the plug-in quotient diagnostic. The nonroutine object is a cone-angle certificate: when the target-loading distance to the estimated bridge nullspace is separated from zero, ordinary Gaussian inference is valid; when it is n^{-1/2}-local, the diagnostic has a nonstandard projected-Gaussian/chi-bar-square law and naive quotient-identification decisions have nonvanishing size distortion. Why promising? This is estimator geometry around a published identification criterion, with a two-matrix hand witness and explicit phase boundary, not LP duality or support coverage. If the delta reduces to standard singular-value perturbation, generic weak-IV analogies, path derivatives, or rephrasing the Zhang nullspace iff, pivot or accept field-tier.
proposed_from.novelty_justification: {"repo_axis":"The static quotient criterion is no longer claimed as novel; it is now Lemma 1. The closest in-catalogue runs are failed eid_proximal_bridge_quotient_v1, downgraded eid_proximal_phase_v1, and failed eid_proximal_completeness_v1. Those runs contain the known nullspace criterion, finite witness language, and weak/nullspace phase aspirations, but not a defined Pi_N/Dkappa local experiment with estimated operator and estimated loading, nor a post-diagnostic size-distortion theorem.","published_axis":"Zhang et al. 2023 cover the population target-uniqueness criterion; Bennett et al. 2023 cover normality under strong functional identification; Kleibergen-Paap 2006 and Robin-Smith 2000 cover rank tests; Andrews-Cheng and Han-McCloskey cover generic weak or singular identification. The epsilon-gap is the proximal quotient statistic in which the estimated nullspace and estimated target loading jointly determine the local projected-Gaussian law and the failure of naive proximal pretest-then-Wald inference."}
seed_list: Introduce a cone-angle quotient certificate for finite proximal bridges and conjecture separated Gaussian versus n^{-1/2}-local projected-Gaussian laws for the plug-in diagnostic.
Construct a uniformly valid post-diagnostic proximal confidence rule that dominates pretest-then-Wald after quotient screening.
Define a target-relevant singular strength variable and conjecture it is the phase boundary between source-condition normality and local quotient limits.
Classify minimal 2-by-2 and 3-by-3 finite negative-control matrices separating unique bridge, quotient-identified target, and non-singleton target phases.
Characterize which algebraic bridge-null directions are realizable by latent negative-control laws satisfying exclusion restrictions.
Give a rank-selection-adjusted cone diagnostic whose chi-bar-square weights are computable from the selected proximal nullspace cone.
Compare proximal AIPW and plug-in quotient decisions and derive the threshold where their diagnostic laws diverge despite the same population target.
Build a finite categorical negative-control witness showing rank failure alone does not decide target uniqueness or diagnostic regularity.
Derive a bootstrap failure theorem for naive resampling of the proximal quotient diagnostic at the nullspace boundary.
Develop a source-condition-to-cone bridge showing when Bennett-style strong functional identification implies positive cone-angle separation.

seed_details.one_line: Introduce a cone-angle quotient certificate for finite proximal bridges and conjecture separated Gaussian versus n^{-1/2}-local projected-Gaussian laws for the plug-in diagnostic.

anchor_paper.author: CausalSmith prior proposal bank
anchor_paper.claim: Static quotient criterion was already known; fixed-rank near-nullspace diagnostics remain open.
anchor_paper.verbatim_quote: derive fixed-rank asymptotic behavior and diagnostics for near-nullspace violations

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Weakens uniqueness only if it creates the phase law; not a new completeness threshold.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from Zhang et al. plus standard singular-value perturbation.
seed_details.rebuttal: The focal object is target-loading projection onto an estimated bridge nullspace, not rank or the static null-annihilator iff.
seed_details.strict_delta: This is not zhang2023proximalWithoutUniqueness because it treats the sampling law of kappa(Ahat,ellhat) under local loading drift.
seed_details.routine_collapse_check: PASS: routine perturbation tools produce a new cone-angle certificate and boundary law, not a domain reuse.

adversarial_referee.instance: Certificate could be only symbolic without a worked finite matrix.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Rebutted by Exhibit 9.1 two-by-two computation of kappa from Ahat and ell.

adversarial_referee.instance: Zhang target may be inference, not just static identification.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Narrowed gap to plug-in quotient diagnostic and local nullspace phase.

adversarial_referee.instance: Two-by-two witness may be hand-tuned at rank one.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Witness is noncorner with h and Gaussian row perturbation; conjecture covers fixed-rank neighborhoods.

seed_details.burned_reason: Angle 0 stayed field-tier through two versions; reviewers found the population criterion already known, comparator anchors thin, Pi_N/Dkappa underdefined, and the local cone-angle law insufficient for flagship novelty.

seed_details.one_line: Construct a uniformly valid post-diagnostic proximal confidence rule that dominates pretest-then-Wald after quotient screening.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Sensitivity/pretest seed is stronger only with explicit critical values across regimes.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is Andrews-Cheng weak-identification inference with proximal names.
seed_details.rebuttal: The pretest statistic is a quotient cone angle built from a bridge nullspace and target loading, not a GMM Jacobian rank parameter.
seed_details.strict_delta: This is not andrewsCheng2012weakSemiStrongStrong because the identification-strength classifier is an estimated proximal quotient projector.
seed_details.routine_collapse_check: PASS if critical values are derived from the cone projection; otherwise downgrade.

adversarial_referee.instance: Uniform confidence rule may be less focal than the diagnostic phase.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Use as second angle after the phase theorem, not chosen v1.

adversarial_referee.instance: Weak-ID literature may already have pretest-size results.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Existing results lack proximal bridge nullspace/loading geometry.
Needs nonvanishing distortion theorem for naive rule plus calibrated alternative.

seed_details.rebuttal: The scalar is the projected target-loading distance after nullspace profiling, with local distributional consequences.

adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Needs necessity/phase boundary; not chosen as primary.

adversarial_referee.instance: Strength variable may be assumed, not computed.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Would need a small-matrix exhibit like Exhibit 9.1.

adversarial_referee.instance: Bennett may already cover regular side.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: The novelty is the local quotient boundary, not regular normality.

seed_details.one_line: Classify minimal 2-by-2 and 3-by-3 finite negative-control matrices separating unique bridge, quotient-identified target, and non-singleton target phases.

proposed_from.literature_map: Stage -1.1 gaps subsume the broad scan. Zhang-Li-Miao-Tchetgen Tchetgen 2023 is the closest proximal anchor: nonunique bridges and root-n inference for functionals. Bennett-Kallus-Mao-Newey-Syrgkanis-Uehara 2023 gives strongly identified functionals under weak/nonunique nuisance bridges. Bennett et al. 2023 source-condition DR inference gives normality on the regular side. Kleibergen-Paap 2006 and Robin-Smith 2000 give estimated-rank tests, but not target-loading distance to a bridge nullspace. Andrews-Cheng 2012/2014 and Han-McCloskey 2019 give weak/singular drifting-sequence inference. Andrews 1999 and Drton 2009 give boundary/tangent-cone geometry. Shi-Miao-Nelson-Tchetgen Tchetgen 2020 and Miao-Shi-Li-Tchetgen Tchetgen 2024 provide finite negative-control matrix settings. Ghassami-Shpitser-Tchetgen Tchetgen 2023 separates failed completeness from quotient-identified targets. The live gap is estimated-operator/estimated-loading local-to-nullspace diagnostic calibration, not the Zhang static nullspace iff.

banked_reason: Stopped early after repeated D-0.5 field-tier reviews: the proximal quotient inference topic was coherent but remained an incremental rank/weak-identification diagnostic around the already-known Zhang null-annihilator criterion, without a flagship comparator-facing uniform inference theorem.
